comments.author: @johnmehaffey9953
comments.text: Poland is on my bucket list, think I’ll fly into Berlin and get the train through Poland stopping off when I see a place I like, don’t like the touristy stuff prefer to go where the locals go, luckily enough my wife works with a polish guy and we’ll get some tips of where to avoid and where to go

replies.author: @martinnndamgarizz
replies.text: I also like to travel and I will try to travel.

replies.author: @johnmehaffey9953
replies.text: @martinnndamgarizzI’m quite a bit older but I traveled a lot being in construction but only in the UK and Europe, I did a lot of moving about when I was younger with work so got to see quite a bit of Europe and especially the UK, good thing was that the company paid my hotel bills,  bad things, I was away from family and friends but bills had to be paid and I got a lot of pleasure visiting different places and especially the people, if you get the opportunity to visit especially through work grab it but make sure it’s a reputable business, 17:48 here now in Ireland so I’m off to get something to eat , must be early in the morning in India, nice communicating with you, as they say in Germany schuss, meaning goodbye

replies.author: @martinnndamgarizz
replies.text:
​@johnmehaffey9953 bro I loved you reply thanks for love .  
I am 22 ur old  You are the first foreigner for me whom  i talking with

replies.author: @johnmehaffey9953
replies.text: @martinnndamgarizzbit far away for me but India has a lot of culture and the worlds biggest democracy, if I was ever to visit it would involve around the mountain railways absolutely brilliant engineering and of course not as much heat in the highlands, I don’t particularly like going where the tourists go as the sites get very crowded , I try  to go where the locals tend to eat and drink, I’ve met a few people from India who have settled here in Ireland and apart from our very changeable weather they love Ireland and it’s people, enjoy your travels

replies.author: @martinnndamgarizz
replies.text: ​@johnmehaffey9953 i will visit Ireland  bro  i am indian and i love European culture very much ( what do you think about India?

replies.author: @johnmehaffey9953
replies.text: @martinnndamgarizzA lot to live up to with the many literary icons born in the Emerald Isle, Swift, Wilde, Joyce, Yeats, Becket to name just a few, some of us are just average people who love life and enjoy the beauty of Ireland, if ever you get the opportunity to visit Ireland you won’t be disappointed a few world heritage sites and outstanding coastlines and beaches, I’d still love to visit Poland and explore other countries in Europe
